prompt: A polynomial P(x) leaves remainder 2 when divided by x-1 and remainder 5 when divided by x-2. What is the remainder when P(x) is divided by (x-1)(x-2)?

rollouts.completion:
<predict>
Solvability: 0.45
Budget: 0.20
</predict>
<think>
Let R=ax+b. Then R(1)=2 and R(2)=5, so a=3,b=-1.
</think>
\boxed{3x-1}

<predict>
Solvability: 0.44
Budget: 0.18
</predict>
<think>
The linear remainder interpolates (1,2) and (2,5), hence slope 3 and intercept -1.
</think>
\boxed{3x-1}

<predict>
Solvability: 0.50
Budget: 0.22
</predict>
<think>
Since P(1)=2 and P(2)=5, the remainder is x+1.
</think>
\boxed{x+1}

<predict>
Solvability: 0.52
Budget: 0.28
</predict>
<think>
Write the remainder as ax+b. Solving gives 3x-1.
</think>
\boxed{3x-1}
